title: MainWindow File UI: implement Menu: File > New > Gate Diagram
description:
﻿Goal:
Implement the first Gate Diagram editor surface reached from File > New > Gate Diagram in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- The File > New > Gate Diagram menu item switches the main content area into gate-diagram editing mode.
- The original modal GATEPICKDLG2 palette is represented as an in-window sidebar instead of a modal/modeless dialog.
- The drawing area is present and rendered as an interactive-ready gate diagram surface.
- Follow-up bd tickets exist for each sidebar button behavior with original source/function references.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added.

Original control inventory:
- Select / move command: 0x42b, forwarded by FUN_0040cabd to the diagram editor.
- Wire command: 0x42c, forwarded by FUN_0040cabd to the diagram editor.
- NOT: command 0x3f4, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 0 with one input.
- 2-In NAND: command 0x3f5, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 1 with two inputs.
- 3-In NAND: command 0x3f6, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 1 with three inputs.
- 4-In NAND: command 0x3f7, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 1 with four inputs.
- 2-In NOR: command 0x3f8, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 2 with two inputs.
- 3-In NOR: command 0x3f9, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 2 with three inputs.
- 4-In NOR: command 0x3fa, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 2 with four inputs.
- 2-In MUX: command 0x3fc, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 5 with three pins.
- 2-In AND: command 0x3fd, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 6 with two inputs.
- 3-In AND: command 0x3fe, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 6 with three inputs.
- 4-In AND: command 0x3ff, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 6 with four inputs.
- 2-In OR: command 0x400, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 7 with two inputs.
- 3-In OR: command 0x401, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 7 with three inputs.
- 4-In OR: command 0x402, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 7 with four inputs.
- Constant 0: command 0x42a, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 10.
- Constant 1: command 0x408, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 11.
- XOR: command 0x430, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 3 with two inputs.
- Input terminal: command 0x438, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 8; naming behavior is in FUN_0040adb4 / FUN_00435e82.
- Output terminal: command 0x439, mapped by FUN_0042af77 to object type 9; naming behavior is in FUN_0040adb4 / FUN_00435e82.
- Submit: command 0x458, mapped by FUN_0040cabd to Enter; FUN_0042b22e calls FUN_00434112 to validate/convert the diagram.
- Cancel: command 0x45a, mapped by FUN_0040cabd to Escape.
- Help: command 0x428, mapped by FUN_0040cabd to main help command 0x801b.

Implementation notes:
- Added GatePaletteItem/GatePaletteKind models for the sidebar command metadata.
- Added MainWindowViewModel gate-diagram state and palette selection.
- Added GateDiagramSurface as the drawing surface; it renders a themed grid/canvas and previews the selected gate-family symbol.
- Added a sidebar in MainWindow.axaml that replaces the original GATEPICKDLG2 modal/modeless palette.
- Created child tickets LogicFriday1-cmz.3.1 through LogicFriday1-cmz.3.24, one per sidebar button.

Source references:
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040cabd_FUN_0040cabd.c: original GATEPICKDLG2 dialog procedure.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042af77_FUN_0042af77.c: gate-palette command ID to object prototype mapping.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042b22e_FUN_0042b22e.c: interactive gate-diagram editor procedure.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00425f03_FUN_00425f03.c: gate symbol drawing logic.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00431daa_FUN_00431daa.c: gate/wire redraw logic.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00434112_FUN_00434112.c: diagram validation/conversion on submit.
- Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv contains concise summaries for these functions.

title: MainWindow File UI: implement Menu: File > New > Truth Table
description:
﻿Goal:
Implement the appropriate behavior/control for File > New > Truth Table in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- The MainWindow File > New > Truth Table menu item opens TruthTableSetupDialog.
- Clicking OK in TruthTableSetupDialog creates and shows a truth table using the Avalonia DataGrid component.
- Input rows are generated for every input combination in binary term order.
- Output cells start at 0, render in blue, and can be cycled by double-click between 0, 1, and X.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added when implemented.

Notes:
Parent analysis ticket: LogicFriday1-cmz.
Use the analysis findings in the parent ticket first. There are decompiled sources and mapping files in .\Decompiled\ and .\Decompiled\logicfriday_decompiled_functions\ that should be used for reference. Avoid using Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled.c directly because it is too large; prefer the per-function files and mapping/reference files.

Implementation notes:
- TruthTableSetupDialog exposes InputNames and OutputNames from the validated setup form.
- MainWindow.NewTruthTable_OnClick reads the setup dialog result and, on OK, calls MainWindowViewModel.StartNewTruthTable().
- MainWindow configures TruthTableDataGrid with dynamic DataGridTextColumn instances for Term, each input name, =>, and each output name.
- TruthTableRows and TruthTableCells are observable model objects bound to the DataGrid cells; the table is not drawn manually.
- Input columns are generated from the term number with the leftmost input as the most significant bit, matching the expected A/B/C/D example.
- Output cells are initialized to 0, rendered in blue through the output DataGrid columns, and double-click cycles each output cell through 0, 1, and X.
- MainWindowViewModel has IsTruthTableVisible and StartNewTruthTable() so the truth-table editor and logic-equation editor are mutually exclusive.

Verification:

Status:
- Implemented locally and awaiting user verification. Do not close until confirmed.

title: MainWindow View Help UI: implement menu behaviors
description:
Goal:
Implement the appropriate behavior and controls that should execute when the user clicks the View and Help menu items in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- View and Help menu items in MainWindow.axaml have implemented click/command behavior where currently missing.
- Existing About behavior is checked against the original application and adjusted if needed.
- Behavior matches the original Logic Friday application as closely as practical.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added when implemented.

Controls to analyze:
- View > Unminimized
- View > Minimized
- Help > Help Contents
- Help > About Logic Friday...

Notes:
There are decompiled sources and mapping files in .\Decompiled\ and .\Decompiled\logicfriday_decompiled_functions\ that should be used for reference. Avoid using Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled.c directly because it is too large; prefer the per-function files and mapping/reference files.

Analysis findings:
Current UI context:
- LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml currently defines View > _Unminimized and View > _Minimized with no Click/Command handler. Help > _Help Contents also has no handler. Help > _About Logic Friday... is wired to AboutLogicFriday_OnClick.

Original View menu behavior:
- View > Unminimized is Win32 command id 0xa2 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2, main window procedure at address 0x004016d2). On WM_INITMENUPOPUP/menu refresh, command 0xa2 is enabled when exactly one function is selected and no busy/edit mode is active; it is radio-checked when selected function field +0x240 / param_1[0x90] is 0. On click, the command sets DAT_0046c1ac[0x90] = 0, then refreshes the selected function/output display through FUN_0040bdc3, FUN_0042093b, FUN_00415a02, starts FUN_0040c130 for output refresh, and rebuilds dependent views via FUN_00417c56, FUN_00417d61, and FUN_00439d71. Intended behavior: switch the selected function display to the unminimized representation, then refresh the displayed function text/truth-table/list UI.
- View > Minimized is Win32 command id 0xa3 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2 at 0x004016d2). On menu refresh, command 0xa3 is enabled only when exactly one function is selected, no busy/edit mode is active, and selected function field +0x23c indicates a minimized representation is available. It is radio-checked when selected function field +0x240 / param_1[0x90] is nonzero. On click, the command sets DAT_0046c1ac[0x90] = 1 and runs the same refresh path as Unminimized. Intended behavior: switch the selected function display to the minimized representation, then refresh the displayed function text/truth-table/list UI.
- The View labels are present in the original resource strings as Decompiled/strings.tsv addresses 0x00471362 "&Unminimized" and 0x0047138a "&Minimized". FUN_00417d61 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00417d61_FUN_00417d61.c uses param_1[0x90] (+0x240) to choose whether to populate the table/display from the unminimized truth-table arrays or from the minimized data arrays, confirming the 0xa2/0xa3 mode switch effect.

Original Help menu behavior:
- Help > Help Contents is Win32 command id 0 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2 at 0x004016d2). The same behavior is also used for WM_HELP (message 0x53). It formats the help target as "%s\\lf.chm::/features.htm" and calls FUN_0043e36f(owner hwnd, target, 0, 0). FUN_0043e36f in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0043e36f_FUN_0043e36f.c (address 0x0043e36f) lazy-loads the HTML Help control from the registry via FUN_0043e408 or falls back to hhctrl.ocx, then dispatches ordinal 0xe, i.e. HtmlHelpA. Intended behavior: open the bundled lf.chm help file to /features.htm using HTML Help with display-topic command 0.
- Help > About Logic Friday... is Win32 command id 0x68 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2 at 0x004016d2). It opens DialogBoxParamA(DAT_00452914, "ABOUTDLG", owner, FUN_0040bfb7, 0). The label is present in Decompiled/strings.tsv at address 0x00471666 "&About Logic Friday...".
- The original About dialog procedure is FUN_0040bfb7 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040bfb7_FUN_0040bfb7.c (address 0x0040bfb7). On WM_INITDIALOG it sets control 0x47d to "Logic Friday v. %d.%d.%d" and initializes hyperlink-style controls. OK/Cancel close the dialog. Commands 0x493 and 0x494 call FUN_004013e5, which opens the email/web targets with ShellExecuteA and shows Logic Friday error boxes if no registered mail app/browser exists. The hyperlink targets are in Decompiled/strings.tsv at 0x0045106c logic.friday@sontrak.com, 0x00451170 mailto:logic.friday@sontrak.com, 0x00451384 www.sontrak.com, and 0x00451488 http://www.sontrak.com. Intended behavior: show the About dialog with version/copyright/tool credits and clickable email/web links.

title: MainWindow Gates UI: implement menu and zoom toolbar behaviors
description:
Goal:
Implement the appropriate behavior and controls that should execute when the user clicks the Gates menu items and related zoom toolbar buttons in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- Gates menu items in MainWindow.axaml have implemented click/command behavior where currently missing.
- Related zoom toolbar buttons have matching behavior and enabled/disabled states.
- Behavior matches the original Logic Friday application as closely as practical.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added when implemented.

Controls to analyze:
- Gates > Modify Gate Diagram
- Gates > Copy to Clipboard
- Gates > IC Package Info...
- Gates > Trace Gate Logic
- Gates > Auto Redraw Diagram
- Gates > Zoom In
- Gates > Zoom Out
- Gates > Zoom All
- Gates > Delete Selected
- Gates > Submit (Enter)
- Gates > Cancel (Esc)
- Toolbar: Zoom In, Zoom Out

Notes:
There are decompiled sources and mapping files in .\Decompiled\ and .\Decompiled\logicfriday_decompiled_functions\ that should be used for reference. Avoid using Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled.c directly because it is too large; prefer the per-function files and mapping/reference files.

Analysis findings:
- Current Avalonia UI declares the Gates menu items without click/command bindings, and the toolbar declares Zoom In/Zoom Out buttons with only tooltips/icons. See LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml lines 109-125 and 204-208.
- Original resource labels for this menu are in Decompiled/strings.tsv at 004710d6-004712ee: &Gates, &Modify Gate Diagram, &Copy to Clipboard, IC &Package Info..., &Trace Gate Logic, &Auto Redraw Diagram, Zoom &Out, &Zoom All, &Delete Selected, and &Submit Enter. The matching earlier &Cancel Esc string is reused for the Cancel item.
- Gates > Modify Gate Diagram maps to main-window command 0x4b1. It is enabled only when exactly one function is selected, that function has a generated/stored gate diagram, the app is not busy, and no edit mode is active. On invoke, the main command handler sets DAT_00452ec8 = 1 and sends command 0xcd to enter the diagram editor for the selected function. Command 0xcd creates the DiagInWindow, shows GATEPICKDLG2, binds variable/gate-label dialogs, and, when DAT_00452ec8 is set, sends custom command 0x8010 to the editor to load the existing diagram. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c lines around command 0x4b1 and case 0xcd; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_0040cabd and FUN_0042b22e.
- Gates > Copy to Clipboard maps to command 0x151. In normal gate-output view it requires exactly one selected function with a gate diagram and calls FUN_0041509f to copy the current gate diagram to the clipboard as enhanced metafile plus bitmap. In active diagram-edit mode it calls FUN_004151da for the editor/generated metafile view. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c around command 0x151; Decompiled/FunctionsMapping.md entries for FUN_0041509f and FUN_004151da.
- Gates > IC Package Info... maps to command 0x156. It requires exactly one selected function with a gate diagram and opens modal dialog ICPKGINFO through FUN_0040aebc. FUN_0040aebc delegates to FUN_0042424d, which displays the precomputed package-count buffer populated by FUN_00423a22. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c around command 0x156; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry for FUN_0040aebc.
- Gates > Trace Gate Logic maps to command 0x152 and behaves as a checked toggle. When off, invoking it creates modeless TRACEDLG16 via FUN_0040ae7a, checks the menu item, and sends DiagOutWindow command 0x8013 to enable trace display. When on, invoking it unchecks the item, sends command 0x8014 to clear trace state/rebuild the EMF, and destroys the trace dialog. It requires exactly one selected function, a gate diagram, no active edit mode, and not busy. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c around command 0x152; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00437b1f_FUN_00437b1f.c commands 0x8013 and 0x8014; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_0040ae7a and FUN_00436c32.
- Gates > Auto Redraw Diagram maps to command 0x15a. It requires exactly one selected function with a gate diagram that can be regenerated, no active edit mode, and not busy. On invoke it calls FUN_00434e03 to prepare/regenerate the mapped diagram layout, sets a dirty/regenerated flag when applicable, then refreshes the display with command 0x801d. Reference: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c around command 0x15a; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry for FUN_00434e03.
- Gates > Zoom In maps to command 0x153. MainWindow forwards this command to the DiagOutWindow. DiagOutWindow expands the current diagram viewport around the client-area center, sets manual-zoom state, redraws, persists viewport bounds, posts WM_SIZE, and refreshes toolbar/menu state. Enabled when a gate-output diagram is visible, no edit/busy mode is active, and DiagOutWindow query command 0x8016 reports zoom-in is possible in the low word. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c menu enable and command forwarding for 0x153; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00437b1f_FUN_00437b1f.c command 0x153 and query 0x8016.
- Gates > Zoom Out maps to command 0x154. MainWindow forwards it to the DiagOutWindow. DiagOutWindow shrinks/expands the viewport toward fitting the diagram, clamps to client bounds, and if the result fits entirely posts Zoom All command 0x155. Enabled when a gate-output diagram is visible, no edit/busy mode is active, and query 0x8016 reports zoom-out/fitting is needed in the high word. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c menu enable and command forwarding for 0x154; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00437b1f_FUN_00437b1f.c command 0x154 and query 0x8016.
- Gates > Zoom All maps to command 0x155. MainWindow forwards it to the DiagOutWindow. DiagOutWindow computes the fit scale from the diagram extents and client size, resets viewport origin to 0, clears manual-zoom state, redraws, persists viewport bounds, posts WM_SIZE, and refreshes command state. Enabled under the same gate-output/no edit/no busy conditions as Zoom Out when query 0x8016 reports the diagram is not already fully fit. Reference: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00437b1f_FUN_00437b1f.c command 0x155.
- Gates > Delete Selected maps to command 0x4b2 during active diagram editing. The main handler sends VK_DELETE (0x2e) to DAT_00452a98/DiagInWindow. FUN_0042b22e handles VK_DELETE by calling FUN_004306d3, which deletes selected gates and selected wire segments, repairs connectivity, redraws, invalidates, and updates the window. Enabled only in diagram-edit mode when deletion is available. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c command 0x4b2; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042b22e_FUN_0042b22e.c VK_DELETE handling; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry for FUN_004306d3.
- Gates > Submit (Enter) maps to command 0x114 in diagram-edit mode, which forwards VK_RETURN (0x0d) to DiagInWindow. FUN_0042b22e handles VK_RETURN by validating/compiling the entered gate diagram with FUN_00434112; on success it updates diagram/package metadata, posts main command 0x800f to finish/commit the edit mode, and on validation failure shows either generic errors or FUN_00435ac0 diagram errors. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c command 0x114; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042b22e_FUN_0042b22e.c VK_RETURN handling; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_00434112 and FUN_00435ac0.
- Gates > Cancel (Esc) maps to command 0x158 in diagram-edit mode and also to VK_ESCAPE inside DiagInWindow. FUN_0042b22e sends main command 0x158 on Escape. The main command handler exits active edit modes; for diagram editing it clears DAT_00452e94, destroys the gate palette and DiagInWindow, reselects the function, clears the modify-existing flag DAT_00452ec8, relayouts, and updates status. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042b22e_FUN_0042b22e.c VK_ESCAPE handling; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c command 0x158 cleanup path.
- Toolbar Zoom In and Toolbar Zoom Out use the same command IDs and behavior as Gates > Zoom In (0x153) and Gates > Zoom Out (0x154). Toolbar enabled state is refreshed by FUN_0040cf1c, which calls DiagOutWindow query 0x8016 and enables toolbar buttons 0x153/0x154 only when a gate-output diagram is visible, no edit mode is active, no long-running operation is busy, and the corresponding zoom direction is possible. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c calls to FUN_0040d2b9 for 0x153 and 0x154; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry for FUN_0040d2b9.

Analysis findings:
Current UI references: LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml defines the target Equation menu controls at lines 90-106 and edit toolbar buttons at lines 170-186. The original captions are present in Decompiled/strings.tsv at addresses 00470e6a (&Modify Equation), 00470eaa (Sum of Produc&ts), 00470eda (Pro&duct of Sums), 00470f0a (&Factor), 00471016 (&Delete Del), 0047103e (Select &All Ctrl+A), and 00471082 (&Submit Enter); the same menu group is handled by the main command dispatcher in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2, WM_COMMAND).

Intended behavior per control:
- Equation > Modify Equation: command 0xb6. Sets DAT_00452ecc = 1 and dispatches command 0x82 to enter logic-equation editing for the selected function. Command 0x82 sets the equation-entry mode flag DAT_00452e98, prepares the RichEdit equation control, loads the selected function equation text when modifying, focuses the equation RichEdit, updates layout, and shows the status prompt. Enabled only when exactly one function is selected, no busy operation is active, and no edit mode is active. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xb6 and command 0x82; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_004016d2 and FUN_00415a02.
- Equation > Sum of Products: command 0x14d. Sets the selected function equation format field at model offset +0x264 to 7, sets status to "Setting equation...", regenerates displayed equation text through FUN_0042093b, then either returns to display state and refreshes the equation output asynchronously or continues the active equation-test workflow. Enabled for a single selected function when not busy, not editing, and the function is eligible for regeneration. References: 004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command range 0x14d-0x14e; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042093b_FUN_0042093b.c; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004209b2_FUN_004209b2.c; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_0042093b and FUN_004209b2.
- Equation > Product of Sums: command 0x14e. Sets equation format field +0x264 to 8. If the selected function has minimized SOP data but lacks minimized POS data, prepares PLA data and launches Espresso before regenerating; otherwise calls FUN_0042093b directly. Generated text is produced by FUN_00421093 and may use a cached minimized POS block. Enabled under the same conditions as Sum of Products. References: 004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x14e; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00421093_FUN_00421093.c; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_0042093b and FUN_00421093.
- Equation > Factor: command 0x4b0. For one selected function, prepares MISII input and launches `misii -x -c "rp -s %s\minin.dat";pf` on a worker thread, setting the factoring busy flag DAT_00452eb0. Completion imports factored output with FUN_0041e1a4, whose text begins with "Factored:\n". Enabled under the same selected/not-busy/not-editing eligibility as the other equation generation commands. References: 004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x4b0 and command-completion 0x8006 path; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0041e1a4_FUN_0041e1a4.c; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry for FUN_0041e1a4.
- Equation > Undo (Ctrl+Z): command 0xb3. Sends EM_UNDO (message 0x00c7 / decimal 199) to the RichEdit equation control DAT_00452ab0. Enabled only in equation-entry mode (DAT_00452e98), when RichEdit reports EM_CANUNDO, and no busy operation is active. - Equation > Redo (Ctrl+Y): command 0xb4. Sends RichEdit EM_REDO (0x454) to DAT_00452ab0. Enabled only in equation-entry mode, when RichEdit reports EM_CANREDO, and not busy. References: 004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xb4; 00439b85_FUN_00439b85.c; 0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c.
- Equation > Cut (Ctrl+X): command 0xad. Sends WM_CUT (0x301) to the RichEdit equation control. Enabled when the RichEdit has a nonempty selection, no busy operation is active, and neither truth-table nor gate-diagram edit mode is active; toolbar Cut uses the same command ID and selection/busy state. Uses custom paste helper FUN_00439af4, which opens the clipboard, reads CF_TEXT, copies the text into allocated memory, inserts it with EM_REPLACESEL, frees memory, and restores editable formatting/caret state through FUN_00439a35. Enabled during equation-entry mode when CF_TEXT is available and not busy. Ctrl+V is intercepted by the RichEdit subclass and posts this command instead of default paste. References: 004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xae; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00439af4_FUN_00439af4.c; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040b12d_FUN_0040b12d.c (Ctrl+V posts 0xae); 00439b85_FUN_00439b85.c clipboard state; 0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c toolbar enable command 0xae.

The submit path reads equation text from the RichEdit into DAT_0045bf50, recognizes hidden debug commands, requires a semicolon, validates characters via FUN_0040dc71, normalizes CR characters via FUN_0040daaf, and for multi-output text writes minin.dat and launches MISII; otherwise it proceeds through the equation parse/set flow. Escape in the RichEdit subclass sends command 0x150. Menu Cancel is enabled while DAT_00452e98 equation-entry mode is active. References: 004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x150 / switchD_004034a5_caseD_b7; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040b12d_FUN_0040b12d.c (Escape handling); Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040e68a_FUN_0040e68a.c shows cancel confirmation helper for switching workflows while equation entry is active.
- Toolbar Cut, Copy, Paste, Undo, Redo: toolbar is created by FUN_0040cd0c from the toolbar table and uses the same command IDs as the Equation edit commands: Cut 0xad, Copy 0xcb, Paste 0xae, Undo 0xb3, Redo 0xb4. Enabled/disabled state is refreshed by FUN_0040cf1c using FUN_00439b85 RichEdit state: Cut requires selection and not busy; Copy requires equation-entry mode + selection + not busy; Paste requires equation-entry mode + CF_TEXT clipboard + not busy; Undo requires equation-entry mode + EM_CANUNDO + not busy; Redo requires equation-entry mode + EM_CANREDO + not busy. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040cd0c_FUN_0040cd0c.c; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00439b85_FUN_00439b85.c; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_0040cd0c and FUN_0040cf1c.

title: MainWindow Truthtable UI: implement menu behaviors
description:
Goal:
Implement the appropriate behavior and controls that should execute when the user clicks the Truthtable menu items in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- Truthtable menu items in MainWindow.axaml have implemented click/command behavior where currently missing.
- Menu enabled/disabled states reflect the current document/editor mode.
- Behavior matches the original Logic Friday application as closely as practical.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added when implemented.

Controls to analyze:
- Truthtable > Modify Truth Table
- Truthtable > Show True and Don't Care
- Truthtable > Show All Rows
- Truthtable > Select All
- Truthtable > True
- Truthtable > False
- Truthtable > Don't Care
- Truthtable > Invert
- Truthtable > Submit (Enter)
- Truthtable > Cancel (Esc)

Notes:
There are decompiled sources and mapping files in .\Decompiled\ and .\Decompiled\logicfriday_decompiled_functions\ that should be used for reference. Avoid using Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled.c directly because it is too large; prefer the per-function files and mapping/reference files.

Analysis findings:
Current UI: LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml lines 72-86 defines the Truthtable menu items but no click/command handlers are wired for them.

Original menu labels: Decompiled/strings.tsv contains the original Truthtable menu label block at 00470c32-00470e1a, including Modify Truth Table, Show True and Don't Care, Show All Rows, Select All, Don't Care, Invert, Submit Enter, and Cancel Esc. True/False are represented by adjacent menu entries in the original resource block; runtime command behavior below identifies their command IDs.

Intended behavior per control:
- Modify Truth Table: command 0x14c. Enabled only when exactly one function is selected, the app is not busy, and no edit mode blocks modification. Handler sets DAT_00452ec4 = 1 and sends command 0x83, which opens TTSETUP; after OK it enters truth-table entry mode and populates the editable truth-table staging data. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c function 004016d2, enable block for 0x14c near lines 704-709, command 0x14c near lines 2702-2704, TTSETUP command 0x83 near lines 991-1020; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry FUN_0040ad29.
- Show True and Don't Care: command 0x161. Enabled for one selected, unminimized function when not busy/editing. Sets selected function field +0x248 (DAT_0046c1ac[0x92]) to 0, refreshes the truth-table output view, and radio-checks this item. In this mode, FUN_00417d61 includes only rows where at least one output value is nonzero, i.e. true (1) or don't-care (2). References: 004016d2 command block at lines 962-979 and radio/check enable block at lines 725-739; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00417d61_FUN_00417d61.c function 00417d61 filtering loop near lines 22-36.
- Show All Rows: command 0x162. Same enablement as 0x161. Sets selected function field +0x248 (DAT_0046c1ac[0x92]) to 1, refreshes the output view, and radio-checks this item; FUN_00417d61 then includes all rows regardless of output value. References: 004016d2 command block at lines 962-979 and radio/check enable block at lines 725-739; 00417d61 filtering loop near lines 22-36.
- Select All: command 0x4b4. Enabled only while truth-table entry mode DAT_00452e90 is active. Selects every row in the editable truth-table ListView and focuses it. References: 004016d2 enable block for 0x4b4 near lines 712-718 and command handler near lines 2702-2704; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0041a350_FUN_0041a350.c function 0041a350.
- True: command 0xe0. Enabled only while truth-table entry mode is active and at least one row is selected. For each selected row and each unlocked output column, writes output value 1, then invalidates/updates the editable ListView. References: 004016d2 enable block for 0xe0 near lines 712-715; command handler case 0xe0 near lines 1664-1676; row selection helper 0041a306 and output lock helper 0041a338.
- False: command 0xe1. Same enablement as True. For each selected row and each unlocked output column, writes output value 0, then refreshes the editable ListView. References: 004016d2 case 0xe1 near lines 1677-1689; helpers 0041a306/0041a338.
- Don't Care: command 0xe2. Same enablement as True. For each selected row and each unlocked output column, writes output value 2, rendered/exported as X/don't-care. References: 004016d2 case 0xe2 near lines 1690-1702; 004198c3 displays value 2 as DAT_0044add0; CSV export 00410a47 writes value 2 as DAT_0044add0.
- Invert: command 0xe4. Same enablement as True. For each selected row and each unlocked output column, toggles 1 to 0 and 0 to 1; value 2/don't-care is left unchanged. References: 004016d2 case 0xe4 near lines 1703-1720; helpers 0041a306/0041a338.
- Submit (Enter): command 0xe5; the editable ListView NM_RETURN notification sends command 0x8002 and reaches the same submit label. Enabled only while truth-table entry mode is active. Submit copies the staged truth table into the target/new function, regenerates equation text with label "Entered by truthtable:", switches out of entry mode, selects/refreshes the function, and updates the display. References: 004016d2 enable block for 0xe5 near lines 716-717, case 0xe5 jump near lines 1721-1722, submit label switchD_004034a5_caseD_e5 near lines 2652-2692, 0x8002 routing near lines 2458-2460; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004198c3_FUN_004198c3.c sends 0x8002 on NM_RETURN; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0041e0c1_FUN_0041e0c1.c copies truth-table data and calls FUN_0042093b(..., "Entered by truthtable:").
- Cancel (Esc): command 0xe6. Enabled only while truth-table entry mode is active. Directly exits truth-table entry mode, clears the modify flag DAT_00452ec4 and truth-table-entry flag DAT_00452e90, refreshes layout/status, and does not apply the staged truth table. Escape in the editable ListView sends 0xe6 directly. The generic Operation > Cancel path uses FUN_0040e68a to ask "Do you want to cancel truth table entry?" and then sends 0xe6 if the user confirms. References: 004016d2 enable block for 0xe6 near lines 716-717 and cancel label switchD_004034a5_caseD_b7 near lines 3164-3208; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040b3c2_FUN_0040b3c2.c Escape sends 0xe6; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040e68a_FUN_0040e68a.c confirmation path.

Additional original behavior notes:
- Editable truth-table double-click cycles a single unlocked output cell 0 -> 1 -> 2 -> 0; column clicks can toggle output-column lock flags, and locked columns are skipped by the bulk True/False/Don't Care/Invert commands. Reference: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004198c3_FUN_004198c3.c function 004198c3.
- Top-level menu enablement for bulk edit commands depends on FUN_0041a2e8 selected-row count; command handlers test row selection with FUN_0041a306 and output-column locks with FUN_0041a338. References: Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries FUN_0041a2e8, FUN_0041a306, FUN_0041a338.

title: MainWindow Operation UI: implement menu and toolbar behaviors
description:
﻿Goal:
Implement the appropriate behavior and controls that should execute when the user clicks the Operation menu items and related toolbar buttons in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- Operation menu items in MainWindow.axaml have implemented click/command behavior where currently missing.
- Related toolbar buttons have matching behavior and enabled/disabled states.
- Behavior matches the original Logic Friday application as closely as practical.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added when implemented.

Controls to analyze:
- Operation > Minimize...
- Operation > Map to Gates...
- Operation > Generate C Lookup Function...
- Operation > Clone Function
- Operation > Compare Functions
- Operation > OR Functions
- Operation > AND Functions
- Operation > XOR Functions
- Operation > Cancel Esc
- Toolbar: Minimize..., Map to Gates...

Notes:
There are decompiled sources and mapping files in .\Decompiled\ and .\Decompiled\logicfriday_decompiled_functions\ that should be used for reference. Avoid using Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled.c directly because it is too large; prefer the per-function files and mapping/reference files.

Analysis findings:
Current UI reference: LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml contains the Operation menu items and toolbar buttons for Minimize... and Map to Gates..., but those controls currently have no Click or Command attributes.

Original command IDs and menu labels:
- Operation menu labels are present in Decompiled/strings.tsv at addresses 00470a26 through 00470c0a: &Operation, &Minimize..., Map to &Gates..., Generate C &Lookup Function..., Clo&ne Function, Compare/OR/AND/XOR Functions, and &Cancel Esc.
- Toolbar labels are present in Decompiled/strings.tsv at 004518ba (Minimize...) and 004518ea (Map to Gates...). Toolbar creation uses the DAT_004516a0 table in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040cd0c_FUN_0040cd0c.c (FUN_0040cd0c), and toolbar tooltip lookup uses the same command table in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040d301_FUN_0040d301.c (FUN_0040d301).

Intended behavior per control:
- Operation > Minimize... uses main command 0x8008 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2). It requires exactly one selected function, warns on estimated long minimization via FUN_0040e1e6 and the MINTIMEDLG dialog handled by FUN_0040e3cf, opens the MINDLG minimization options dialog handled by FUN_0040e469, writes Espresso input via FUN_00414ab1, launches espresso through FUN_0040c196, and sets the minimization busy state. Mapping summary: Decompiled/FunctionsMapping.md lists main command 0x8008 as Start minimization.
- Toolbar Minimize... should invoke the same 0x8008 command. Its enabled state is refreshed in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c (FUN_0040cf1c): enabled only when exactly one selected function exists, no busy/edit operation is active, and the selected function is not already in the disqualifying minimized/busy state checked at model offset +0x250. Menu enabled state is refreshed in FUN_004016d2 via FUN_0040ca01 for command 0x8008 with the same one-selection/no-busy/no-edit/no-disqualifying-state conditions.
- Operation > Map to Gates... uses main command 0xa5 in FUN_004016d2. It requires exactly one selected function. If the function is not trivially all-true/all-false, it may warn on expensive mapping via FUN_0040dd2e and MAPTIME handled by FUN_0040e14c, opens MAPDIALOG through FUN_0040ad72, writes gate-library options through FUN_00422090, ensures minimization exists by posting 0x8008 if needed, then launches MISII through FUN_0040c196 and sets the gate-mapping busy state. If the function is all true or all false, it shows a message that no gates are required. Mapping summary: Decompiled/FunctionsMapping.md lists main command 0xa5 as Start gate mapping.
- Toolbar Map to Gates... should invoke the same 0xa5 command. Its enabled state is refreshed in FUN_0040cf1c: enabled only when exactly one selected function exists and no busy/edit operation is active. Menu enabled state is refreshed in FUN_004016d2 via FUN_0040ca01 for command 0xa5 with the same one-selection/no-busy/no-edit conditions.
- Operation > Generate C Lookup Function... uses command 0xc2 in FUN_004016d2. It requires exactly one selected function, stores the selected function input count in _DAT_004519b0, opens LOOKUPFCNDLG via FUN_0040afa6/FUN_004170c1, asks for a "lookup" save target via FUN_00410476, then writes C lookup-table source through FUN_004133f5 and opens the result in Notepad. Function details are summarized in Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv for FUN_0040afa6, FUN_004170c1, and FUN_004133f5. Menu enabled state in FUN_004016d2 command 0xc2 requires exactly one selected function, no busy operation, no edit mode, and model offset +0x250 equal to 0.
- Operation > Clone Function uses command 0xde in FUN_004016d2. It requires exactly one selected function, calls FUN_0040b628 to duplicate it into a new function-list slot, selects/adds the cloned entry with FUN_0040eb81, and refreshes/binds the selected model with FUN_0040f38f. FUN_0040b628 is in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040b628_FUN_0040b628.c and uses FUN_0041cbd4 to clone the function model. Menu enabled state requires exactly one selected function, no busy/edit operation, and model offset +0x250 equal to 0.
- Operation > Compare Functions uses command 0xbb in FUN_004016d2. It requires exactly two selected functions, obtains them with FUN_0040f234, calls FUN_00415dd9, and may create a new result function if the comparison dialog requests it. FUN_00415dd9 is in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00415dd9_FUN_00415dd9.c and validates same input count/single output, reconciles input-name ordering, classifies equality/subset/complement/inequality, and opens COMPRESDLG through FUN_0040af6c. Menu enabled state requires exactly two selected functions and no busy/edit operation.
- Operation > OR Functions uses command 0xbc in FUN_004016d2. It requires exactly two selected one-output compatible functions and calls FUN_00416a26 with command 0xbc. FUN_00416a26 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00416a26_FUN_00416a26.c validates same input count and one output, handles variable-name ordering, prompts for a new function name, then produces row-wise OR while preserving don't-care value 2 where appropriate. If a result is requested, FUN_004016d2 allocates/binds a new function and refreshes the list. Enabled when exactly two functions are selected and no busy/edit operation is active.
- Operation > AND Functions uses command 0xbd in FUN_004016d2 and the same FUN_00416a26 workflow, but applies row-wise AND semantics with don't-care preservation. Enabled under the same exactly-two-functions/no-busy/no-edit condition.
- Operation > XOR Functions uses command 0xc0 in FUN_004016d2 and the same FUN_00416a26 workflow, but applies row-wise XOR semantics; any row containing don't-care 2 produces don't-care 2. Enabled under the same exactly-two-functions/no-busy/no-edit condition.
- Operation > Cancel Esc uses command 0xb7 in FUN_004016d2. It is enabled when a long-running operation is active. The handler label switchD_004034a5_caseD_b7 sets the cancellation event DAT_0046c2c8 when minimization/gate mapping/factoring/other busy flags are active, exits equation/truth-table/gate edit modes as applicable, destroys the gate edit palette/windows, clears edit-mode flags, refreshes layout via FUN_00439d71, updates status via FUN_0040bdc3, and refreshes toolbar state via FUN_0040cf1c. Menu enabled state is set in FUN_004016d2 via FUN_0040ca01 for command 0xb7 using the busy flag computed from DAT_00452e7c, DAT_00452eb4, DAT_00452eb0, and DAT_00452ed8.

comments.text: Dependency tracking rule: do not encode bead dependency metadata in Rust source files. Required SIS/port prerequisite relationships must be represented with Beads dependency edges using `bd dep` / `bd blocked`; source code should contain only runtime behavior and diagnostics.

title: MainWindow File UI: implement menu and toolbar behaviors
description:
Goal:
Implement the appropriate behavior and controls that should execute when the user clicks the File menu items and related toolbar buttons in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- File menu items in MainWindow.axaml have implemented click/command behavior where currently missing.
- Related toolbar buttons have matching behavior and enabled/disabled states.
- Behavior matches the original Logic Friday application as closely as practical.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added when implemented.

Controls to analyze:
- File > New > Truth Table
- File > New > Logic Equation
- File > New > Gate Diagram
- File > Open...
- File > Close
- File > Save As...
- File > Import Truth Table...
- File > Export Truth Table...
- File > Export Gate Diagram...
- File > Print...
- File > Exit
- Toolbar: New..., Open..., Save, Print

Notes:
There are decompiled sources and mapping files in .\Decompiled\ and .\Decompiled\logicfriday_decompiled_functions\ that should be used for reference. Avoid using Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled.c directly because it is too large; prefer the per-function files and mapping/reference files.

Analysis findings:
Current UI reference: LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml contains the File menu and toolbar buttons currently needing behavior. Original menu labels are present in Decompiled/strings.tsv at 004707ae-004709d6.

Original command IDs and behavior:
- File > New > Truth Table: command 0x83. Enabled only when not busy and not already in an edit mode. Opens TTSETUP, and on OK initializes a new truth-table editing state/view from the setup data, clears any existing truth-table edit child window, updates menus/status, and redraws. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x83; enable logic also in FUN_004016d2 menu-update block.
- File > New > Logic Equation: command 0x82. Enabled only when not busy and not already in an edit mode. Enters equation-input/edit mode for a new function, clears the equation editor state, destroys the truth-table edit child window if present, and updates toolbar/menu state. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x82; enable logic in FUN_004016d2 menu-update block.
- File > New > Gate Diagram: command 0xcd. Enabled only when not busy and not already in an edit mode. Allocates/uses a function slot, creates the DiagInWindow gate-diagram editor, shows gate picker dialogs, initializes gate editing helper procedures, and sets the app into gate-diagram edit mode. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xcd; resource label in Decompiled/strings.tsv at 00470842.
- Toolbar New...: command 0x81. Enabled only when not busy and not in any edit mode. Opens NEWFCNDLG and dispatches the user's choice: result 0 -> command 0x83 Truth Table, result 1 -> command 0x82 Logic Equation, result 2 -> command 0xcd Gate Diagram. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x81; dialog proc Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040ca22_FUN_0040ca22.c; toolbar enable in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c; toolbar text lookup in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040d301_FUN_0040d301.c and Decompiled/strings.tsv at 004516aa.
- File > Open... and toolbar Open...: command 0xa8. Enabled when no long-running operation is active. Prepares a File Open caption/context, shows an Open dialog filtered to Logic function (*.lfcn), allocates a function slot, loads the .lfcn file, stores the selected path/name, adds it to the function list, restores truth table/gate state, and refreshes views. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xa8 / 0x8023 path; open dialog helper Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004102f4_FUN_004102f4.c; load implementation Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00412923_FUN_00412923.c; toolbar enable in 0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c.
- File > Close: command 0xab. Enabled when at least one function is selected and no long-running operation/edit mode is active. Iterates selected functions, prompts "Function %s has been changed. Do you want to save it?" for dirty functions, routes Yes through command 0xa9 Save, aborts on Cancel, then removes each selected function and selects the previous remaining function or clears views when none remain. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xab.
- Toolbar Save: command 0xa9. Enabled when at least one function is selected and no long-running operation/edit mode is active. Requires exactly one selected function at execution; if it has an existing path, writes the Logic Friday .lfcn file through FUN_00412219. If there is no existing path, or save returns the special Save As-needed error 0x1d0001, it dispatches to command 0xaa Save As. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xa9; save implementation Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00412219_FUN_00412219.c; toolbar enable in 0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c.
- File > Save As...: command 0xaa. Enabled only for exactly one selected function and no long-running operation/edit mode. Builds a default filename from the existing path or function/output names, shows a Save dialog filtered to Logic function (*.lfcn), writes via FUN_00412219, and stores the chosen path on success. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xaa; dialog helper Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004103b5_FUN_004103b5.c; save implementation 00412219_FUN_00412219.c.
- File > Import Truth Table...: command 0x4b3. Enabled when no long-running operation/edit mode is active, independent of current selection. Prepares Import Truth Table context, shows an Open dialog filtered to All Files (*.*), allocates a new function slot, imports/parses the selected text/CSV truth table, annotates the function with "Imported from file:", displays the truth-table view, and refreshes function list/menu state. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x4b3; dialog helper Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004105fb_FUN_004105fb.c; import implementation Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004110a2_FUN_004110a2.c; import error messages in 00411f69_FUN_00411f69.c.
- File > Export Truth Table...: command 0x144. Enabled only for exactly one selected function and no long-running operation/edit mode. Builds a default CSV name from function/output names, optionally shows TTEXPORTWARN for certain reduced/multi-output exports, shows a Save dialog filtered to Comma Separated Variables (*.csv), and writes input/output names and rows with 0/1/X values. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x144; dialog helper Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004106be_FUN_004106be.c; export implementation Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00410a47_FUN_00410a47.c.
- File > Export Gate Diagram...: command 0xcc. Enabled only for exactly one selected function that has a generated gate diagram and no long-running operation/edit mode. Builds a default name, shows a Save dialog filtered to Enhanced Metafile (*.emf), then exports through FUN_00410780. FUN_00410780 copies the enhanced metafile directly for .emf paths; for .bmp or other extensions it renders the diagram to a 1-bit BMP and appends .bmp when needed. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xcc; dialog helper Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00410538_FUN_00410538.c; export implementation Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00410780_FUN_00410780.c.
- File > Print... and toolbar Print: command 0xb9. Enabled only for exactly one selected function, no long-running operation/edit mode, and printing not suppressed by DAT_00452ed0. Executes FUN_0041a687, which opens the custom Logic Friday: Printing dialog, defaults to diagram mode when the selected function has diagram objects, otherwise text/equation mode, then prints either text/equations or the gate diagram with cancel support. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0xb9; print workflow Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0041a687_FUN_0041a687.c, with text printing 0041ac36_FUN_0041ac36.c and diagram printing 0041b260_FUN_0041b260.c; print dialog hook 0040aefe_FUN_0040aefe.c; toolbar enable in 0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c; toolbar text in Decompiled/strings.tsv at 0045173a.
- File > Exit: command 0x69. Posts WM_CLOSE to the main window. The WM_CLOSE handler walks all open functions, prompts to save dirty functions with the same File prompt and command 0xa9 Save path, cancels close on Cancel, otherwise persists settings, releases temp/help resources, relaxes temp-file security, and destroys the window. Source: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c, FUN_004016d2 command 0x69 and WM_CLOSE branch param_2 == 0x10.

Implementation notes:
- The current Avalonia menu has File > Save As... but no File > Save item; original toolbar Save is separate command 0xa9 and should not be wired to Save As directly.
- Original menu and toolbar enabled states are separate: menu enable/disable is in FUN_004016d2's menu update block using FUN_0040ca01; toolbar enable/disable for New/Open/Save/Print is in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040cf1c_FUN_0040cf1c.c using FUN_0040d2b9.

title: docs: complete remaining function mappings
description:
Goal:
